steps.title: Recognize that the solution is the intersection point
steps.content: The solution to a system of linear equations is the point $(x, y)$ where both lines intersect. Looking at the graph, the two lines cross at a single point.

steps.title: Read the coordinates from the graph
steps.content: Locate the intersection point on the grid. Trace from the intersection point straight down to the $x$-axis: it aligns with $x = -4$. Trace from the intersection point straight left to the $y$-axis: it aligns with $y = -3$. Therefore, the solution is $(-4, -3)$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
